fastnear-system-health.description: Ping the FastNEAR API for liveness — returns `{status: ok}` when healthy.

parameters.name: apiKey
parameters.description: Optional API key forwarded by embedded portal clients. The public FastNEAR API does not require it.

fastnear-system-health.response: Health status string Internal Server Error

fastnear-system-status.description: Check the current indexed block height, latency, and deployed service version.

parameters.name: apiKey
parameters.description: Optional API key forwarded by embedded portal clients. The public FastNEAR API does not require it.

fastnear-system-status.response: Current FastNEAR API sync status Internal Server Error

fastnear-v0-account-ft.description: Fetch the fungible token contract IDs an account has held — contract IDs only, no balances.

parameters.name: account_id
parameters.description: NEAR account ID to inspect.

parameters.name: apiKey
parameters.description: Optional API key forwarded by embedded portal clients. The public FastNEAR API does not require it.

fastnear-v0-account-ft.response: Fungible token contract IDs for the requested account Bad Request Internal Server Error

fastnear-v0-account-nft.description: Fetch the NFT contract IDs an account has held — contract IDs only, no block-height metadata.

parameters.name: account_id
parameters.description: NEAR account ID to inspect.

parameters.name: apiKey
parameters.description: Optional API key forwarded by embedded portal clients. The public FastNEAR API does not require it.

fastnear-v0-account-nft.response: NFT contract IDs for the requested account Bad Request Internal Server Error

fastnear-v0-account-staking.description: Fetch staking pool account IDs for one account — pool IDs only, no block-height metadata.

parameters.name: account_id
parameters.description: NEAR account ID to inspect.

parameters.name: apiKey
parameters.description: Optional API key forwarded by embedded portal clients. The public FastNEAR API does not require it.

fastnear-v0-account-staking.response: Staking pool account IDs for the requested account Bad Request Internal Server Error

fastnear-v0-public-key-lookup.description: Fetch the account IDs that have registered a full-access public key, via the legacy V0 lookup path.

parameters.name: public_key
parameters.description: NEAR public key in `ed25519:...` or `secp256k1:...` form.

parameters.name: apiKey
parameters.description: Optional API key forwarded by embedded portal clients. The public FastNEAR API does not require it.

fastnear-v0-public-key-lookup.response: Matching account IDs for the supplied full-access public key Bad Request Internal Server Error

fastnear-v0-public-key-lookup-all.description: List every account tied to a public key — full-access and limited-access keys together — via the legacy V0 lookup-all path.

parameters.name: public_key
parameters.description: NEAR public key in `ed25519:...` or `secp256k1:...` form.

parameters.name: apiKey
parameters.description: Optional API key forwarded by embedded portal clients. The public FastNEAR API does not require it.

fastnear-v0-public-key-lookup-all.response: Matching account IDs for the supplied public key, including limited-access keys Bad Request Internal Server Error

fastnear-v1-account-ft.description: Fetch an account's fungible token balance rows, each with contract ID, balance, and last-update block height.

parameters.name: account_id
parameters.description: NEAR account ID to inspect.

parameters.name: apiKey
parameters.description: Optional API key forwarded by embedded portal clients. The public FastNEAR API does not require it.

fastnear-v1-account-ft.response: Indexed fungible token rows for the requested account Bad Request Internal Server Error

fastnear-v1-account-full.description: Fetch the combined indexed account view, including staking pools, FT balances, NFTs, and account state.

parameters.name: account_id
parameters.description: NEAR account ID to inspect.

parameters.name: apiKey
parameters.description: Optional API key forwarded by embedded portal clients. The public FastNEAR API does not require it.

fastnear-v1-account-full.response: Full indexed account information for the requested account Bad Request Internal Server Error

fastnear-v1-account-nft.description: Fetch NFT contract rows for an account, including block-height metadata for each contract.

parameters.name: account_id
parameters.description: NEAR account ID to inspect.

parameters.name: apiKey
parameters.description: Optional API key forwarded by embedded portal clients. The public FastNEAR API does not require it.

fastnear-v1-account-nft.response: Indexed NFT contract rows for the requested account Bad Request Internal Server Error

fastnear-v1-account-staking.description: Retrieve staking pool rows for an account, including block-height metadata for each pool relationship.

parameters.name: account_id
parameters.description: NEAR account ID to inspect.

parameters.name: apiKey
parameters.description: Optional API key forwarded by embedded portal clients. The public FastNEAR API does not require it.

fastnear-v1-account-staking.response: Indexed staking pool rows for the requested account Bad Request Internal Server Error

fastnear-v1-ft-top.description: Fetch the top-balance holder list for a fungible token contract, ranked highest balance first.

parameters.name: token_id
parameters.description: Fungible token contract account ID.

parameters.name: apiKey
parameters.description: Optional API key forwarded by embedded portal clients. The public FastNEAR API does not require it.

fastnear-v1-ft-top.response: Indexed top holders for the requested fungible token Bad Request Internal Server Error

fastnear-v1-public-key-lookup.description: Resolve a full-access public key to the indexed NEAR accounts that have registered it — V1 canonical lookup path.

parameters.name: public_key
parameters.description: NEAR public key in `ed25519:...` or `secp256k1:...` form.

parameters.name: apiKey
parameters.description: Optional API key forwarded by embedded portal clients. The public FastNEAR API does not require it.

fastnear-v1-public-key-lookup.response: Matching account IDs for the supplied full-access public key Bad Request Internal Server Error

fastnear-v1-public-key-lookup-all.description: Resolve a public key to every account that holds it — full-access and limited-access keys alike — via the V1 lookup-all path.

parameters.name: public_key
parameters.description: NEAR public key in `ed25519:...` or `secp256k1:...` form.

parameters.name: apiKey
parameters.description: Optional API key forwarded by embedded portal clients. The public FastNEAR API does not require it.

fastnear-v1-public-key-lookup-all.response: Matching account IDs for the supplied public key, including limited-access keys Bad Request Internal Server Error

kv-fastdata-v0-all-by-predecessor.description: Fetch the latest values for all contracts touched by one predecessor account.

parameters.name: predecessor_id
parameters.description: Calling account that wrote the FastData keys.

parameters.name: include_metadata
parameters.description: Include receipt and signer metadata in each entry.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of entries to return in one page (1–200, default 50).

parameters.name: page_token
parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor from a previous `/v0/all/{predecessor_id}` response.

kv-fastdata-v0-all-by-predecessor.response: Latest rows for the selected predecessor Invalid key filter or page token Scylla query failure

schema.name: value
schema.description: Raw JSON value as stored in FastData.

schema.name: page_token
schema.description: Opaque pagination cursor for the next page. Absent when there are no more results.

kv-fastdata-v0-get-history-key.description: Fetch every historical write for one exact key under one predecessor and contract.

parameters.name: current_account_id
parameters.description: Contract account whose FastData keys were written.

parameters.name: predecessor_id
parameters.description: Calling account that wrote the FastData keys.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: Exact FastData key to return.

kv-fastdata-v0-get-history-key.response: Historical rows for the selected predecessor, account, and key Scylla query failure

schema.name: value
schema.description: Raw JSON value as stored in FastData.

schema.name: page_token
schema.description: Opaque pagination cursor for the next page. Absent when there are no more results.

kv-fastdata-v0-get-latest-key.description: Fetch the latest value for one exact key under one predecessor and contract.

parameters.name: current_account_id
parameters.description: Contract account whose FastData keys were written.

parameters.name: predecessor_id
parameters.description: Calling account that wrote the FastData keys.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: Exact FastData key to return.

kv-fastdata-v0-get-latest-key.response: Latest rows for the selected predecessor, account, and key Scylla query failure

schema.name: value
schema.description: Raw JSON value as stored in FastData.

schema.name: page_token
schema.description: Opaque pagination cursor for the next page. Absent when there are no more results.

kv-fastdata-v0-history-by-account.description: Fetch historical FastData rows for one target account across all predecessor accounts.

parameters.name: current_account_id
parameters.description: Contract account whose FastData keys were written.

parameters.name: asc
parameters.description: Sort ascending for history endpoints. Defaults to newest-first.

parameters.name: include_metadata
parameters.description: Include receipt and signer metadata in each entry.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: Exact key filter. Mutually exclusive with `key_prefix`.

parameters.name: key_prefix
parameters.description: Prefix filter for matching key namespaces.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of entries to return in one page (1–200, default 50).

parameters.name: page_token
parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor from a previous response for the same endpoint and filter set.

kv-fastdata-v0-history-by-account.response: Historical rows for the selected contract Invalid key filter or page token Scylla query failure

schema.name: value
schema.description: Raw JSON value as stored in FastData.

schema.name: page_token
schema.description: Opaque pagination cursor for the next page. Absent when there are no more results.

kv-fastdata-v0-history-by-key.description: Fetch the historical values for a selected key across accounts and predecessors.

parameters.name: asc
parameters.description: Sort ascending for history results. Defaults to newest-first.

parameters.name: include_metadata
parameters.description: Include receipt and signer metadata in each entry.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: Exact key name to match across all accounts and predecessors.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of entries to return in one page (1–200, default 50).

parameters.name: page_token
parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor from a previous response for the same endpoint and filter set.

kv-fastdata-v0-history-by-key.response: Historical rows for the selected key Invalid key filter or page token Scylla query failure

schema.name: value
schema.description: Raw JSON value as stored in FastData.

schema.name: page_token
schema.description: Opaque pagination cursor for the next page. Absent when there are no more results.

parameters.description: Sort ascending for history endpoints. Defaults to newest-first.

parameters.name: include_metadata

parameters.description: Prefix filter for matching key namespaces.

parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor from a previous response for the same endpoint and filter set.

kv-fastdata-v0-multi.description: Fetch the latest FastData row for up to 100 keys in a single request.

parameters.description: Fully qualified keys in current_account_id/predecessor_id/key form.

neardata-system-health.description: Ping the neardata service for liveness — returns `{status: ok}` when healthy, errors otherwise.

parameters.name: apiKey
parameters.description: Optional FastNEAR subscription API key. Invalid values may return `401` before redirect handling.

neardata-system-health.response: Health payload Invalid or unauthorized API key Cache or internal data error

neardata-v0-block.description: Fetch a finalized block's full document at a chosen height — header plus every chunk and shard payload.

parameters.name: block_height
parameters.description: NEAR block height to retrieve.

parameters.name: apiKey
parameters.description: Optional FastNEAR subscription API key. Invalid values may return `401` before redirect handling.

neardata-v0-block.response: Requested document, or `null` when the selected slice is absent Redirect to a canonical archive or finalized block URL Invalid or unauthorized API key Structured block-height error Cache or internal data error

schema.name: BlockDocument
schema.description: Full block document as served by neardata, including the block envelope and per-shard payloads.

schema.name: block
schema.description: Block-level payload returned by neardata.

schema.name: BlockEnvelope
schema.description: Block-level payload returned by neardata.

schema.name: author
schema.description: Block producer account ID.

schema.name: ChunkHeader
schema.description: Chunk header object as served by neardata.

schema.name: header
schema.description: Block header object as served by neardata.

schema.name: BlockHeader
schema.description: Block header object as served by neardata.

schema.name: ShardDocument
schema.description: Per-shard payload returned by neardata for a block.

schema.name: chunk
schema.description: Chunk payload returned by neardata for a single shard in a selected block.

schema.name: ChunkDocument
schema.description: Chunk payload returned by neardata for a single shard in a selected block.

schema.name: author
schema.description: Chunk producer account ID.

schema.name: header
schema.description: Chunk header object as served by neardata.

schema.name: ReceiptDocument
schema.description: Receipt object as served by neardata inside a chunk payload.

schema.name: ChunkTransactionWrapper
schema.description: Transaction entry returned inside a neardata chunk.

schema.name: outcome
schema.description: Execution result paired with an optional receipt object.

schema.name: ExecutionWithReceipt
schema.description: Execution result paired with an optional receipt object.

schema.name: receipt
schema.description: Receipt payload when neardata includes it for this entry.

schema.name: StateChangeItem
schema.description: State change entry returned by neardata for a shard.

neardata-v0-block-chunk.description: Fetch one chunk — a single shard's transactions and incoming receipts — at a chosen block height.

parameters.name: block_height
parameters.description: NEAR block height to retrieve.

parameters.name: shard_id
parameters.description: Shard ID whose chunk should be returned.

parameters.name: apiKey
parameters.description: Optional FastNEAR subscription API key. Invalid values may return `401` before redirect handling.

neardata-v0-block-chunk.response: Requested document, or `null` when the selected slice is absent Redirect to a canonical archive or finalized block URL Invalid or unauthorized API key Structured block-height error Cache or internal data error

schema.description: Chunk payload returned by neardata for a single shard in a selected block.

schema.name: ChunkTransactionWrapper
schema.description: Transaction entry returned inside a neardata chunk.

schema.name: outcome
schema.description: Execution result paired with an optional receipt object.

schema.name: ExecutionWithReceipt
schema.description: Execution result paired with an optional receipt object.

schema.name: receipt
schema.description: Receipt payload when neardata includes it for this entry.

schema.name: StateChangeItem
schema.description: State change entry returned by neardata for a shard.

rpc-block-by-height.description: Fetch a block's header and chunk summaries by its height in the chain.

parameters.name: block_id
parameters.description: Block height to query

rpc-block-by-id.description: Fetch a block's header and chunk summaries by its Base58-encoded SHA-256 hash.

parameters.name: block_id
parameters.description: Base58-encoded block hash

rpc-block-effects.description: Summarize every state change in a block — which accounts, keys, and contract-state entries were touched.

parameters.name: block_id
parameters.description: Block height (integer) or block hash (string)

schema.name: block_hash
schema.description: Base58-encoded hash

rpc-broadcast-tx-async.description: Submit a freshly signed transaction and immediately get its hash — no wait for execution. Default examples are illustrative only and are not reusable signed payloads.

parameters.name: signed_tx_base64
parameters.description: Base64-encoded signed transaction. Generate a fresh signed payload for the signing account immediately before submission or status lookup.

parameters.name: wait_until
parameters.description: Desired level of execution status guarantee

schema.name: result
schema.description: Base58-encoded hash

rpc-broadcast-tx-commit.description: Submit a freshly signed transaction and wait for its commit — the legacy synchronous send, superseded by `send_tx`. Default examples are illustrative only and are not reusable signed payloads.

parameters.name: signed_tx_base64
parameters.description: Base64-encoded signed transaction. Generate a fresh signed payload for the signing account immediately before submission or status lookup.

parameters.name: wait_until
parameters.description: Desired level of execution status guarantee

schema.name: final_execution_status
schema.description: Desired level of execution status guarantee

schema.name: receipts
schema.description: Receipts generated from the transaction

schema.name: receipts_outcome
schema.description: The execution outcome of receipts.

schema.name: status
schema.description: One of multiple possible types

schema.name: transaction
schema.description: Signed Transaction

schema.name: transaction_outcome
schema.description: The execution outcome of the signed transaction.

rpc-call-function.description: Invoke a contract view method without gas or state changes — reads computed values from contract logic.

parameters.name: account_id
parameters.description: NEAR account ID

parameters.name: args_base64
parameters.description: Base64-encoded raw method arguments. JSON-based contracts typically expect UTF-8 bytes of the JSON payload; the example `e30=` is `{}`.

parameters.name: method_name
parameters.description: Name of the contract view method to invoke.

schema.name: result
schema.description: A result returned by contract method

rpc-changes.description: Fetch detailed state changes in a block — filter by account, key prefix, or change type.

schema.name: block_hash
schema.description: Base58-encoded hash

schema.name: cause
schema.description: One of multiple possible types

rpc-client-config.description: Fetch the node's own local client config — timeouts, retry settings, and operator-chosen parameters.

schema.name: result
schema.description: ClientConfig where some fields can be updated at runtime.

schema.name: archive
schema.description: Not clear old data, set `true` for archive nodes.

schema.name: block_fetch_horizon
schema.description: Horizon at which instead of fetching block, fetch full state.

schema.name: block_header_fetch_horizon
schema.description: Behind this horizon header fetch kicks in.

schema.name: block_production_tracking_delay
schema.description: Duration to check for producing / skipping block.

schema.name: catchup_step_period
schema.description: Time between check to perform catchup.

schema.name: chain_id
schema.description: Chain id for status.

schema.name: chunk_distribution_network
schema.description: Config for the Chunk Distribution Network feature. This allows nodes to push and pull chunks from a central stream. The two benefits of this approach are: (1) less request/response traffic on the peer-to-peer network and (2) lower latency for RPC nodes indexing the chain.

schema.name: uris
schema.description: URIs for the Chunk Distribution Network feature.

schema.name: chunk_request_retry_period
schema.description: Time between checking to re-request chunks.

schema.name: chunk_validation_threads
schema.description: Number of threads for ChunkValidationActor pool.

schema.name: chunk_wait_mult
schema.description: Multiplier for the wait time for all chunks to be received.

schema.name: chunks_cache_height_horizon
schema.description: Height horizon for the chunk cache. A chunk is removed from the cache if its height + chunks_cache_height_horizon < largest_seen_height. The default value is DEFAULT_CHUNKS_CACHE_HEIGHT_HORIZON.

schema.name: client_background_migration_threads
schema.description: Number of threads to execute background migration work in client.

schema.name: cloud_archival_writer
schema.description: Configuration for a cloud-based archival writer. If this config is present, the writer is enabled and writes chunk-related data based on the tracked shards. This config also controls additional archival behavior such as block data and polling interval.

schema.name: archive_block_data
schema.description: Determines whether block-related data should be written to cloud storage.

schema.name: polling_interval
schema.description: Interval at which the system checks for new blocks or chunks to archive.

schema.name: disable_tx_routing
schema.description: If true, the node won't forward transactions to next the chunk producers.

schema.name: doomslug_step_period
schema.description: Time between running doomslug timer.

schema.name: enable_early_prepare_transactions
schema.description: If true, transactions for the next chunk will be prepared early, right after the previous chunk's post-state is ready. This can help produce chunks faster, for high-throughput chains. The current implementation increases latency on low-load chains, which will be fixed in the future. The default is disabled.

schema.name: enable_statistics_export
schema.description: Re-export storage layer statistics as prometheus metrics.

schema.name: epoch_length
schema.description: Epoch length.

schema.name: epoch_sync
schema.description: Options for epoch sync.

schema.name: epoch_sync_horizon_num_epochs
schema.description: Number of epochs behind the network head beyond which the node will use epoch sync instead of header sync. At the consumption site, this is multiplied by epoch_length to get the horizon in blocks.

schema.name: timeout_for_epoch_sync
schema.description: Timeout for epoch sync requests. The node will continue retrying indefinitely even if this timeout is exceeded.

schema.name: expected_shutdown
schema.description: Graceful shutdown at expected block height.

schema.name: gc
schema.description: Configuration for garbage collection.

schema.name: gc_blocks_limit
schema.description: Maximum number of blocks to garbage collect at every garbage collection call.

schema.name: gc_fork_clean_step
schema.description: Maximum number of height to go through at each garbage collection step when cleaning forks during garbage collection.

schema.name: gc_num_epochs_to_keep
schema.description: Number of epochs for which we keep store data.

schema.name: gc_step_period
schema.description: How often gc should be run

schema.name: header_sync_expected_height_per_second
schema.description: Expected increase of header head height per second during header sync

schema.name: header_sync_initial_timeout
schema.description: How much time to wait after initial header sync

schema.name: header_sync_progress_timeout
schema.description: How much time to wait after some progress is made in header sync

schema.name: header_sync_stall_ban_timeout
schema.description: How much time to wait before banning a peer in header sync if sync is too slow

schema.name: log_summary_period
schema.description: Period between logging summary information.

schema.name: log_summary_style
schema.description: Enable coloring of the logs

schema.name: max_block_production_delay
schema.description: Maximum wait for approvals before producing block.

schema.name: max_block_wait_delay
schema.description: Maximum duration before skipping given height.

schema.name: max_gas_burnt_view
schema.description: Gas amount

schema.name: min_block_production_delay
schema.description: Minimum duration before producing block.

schema.name: min_num_peers
schema.description: Minimum number of peers to start syncing.

schema.name: num_block_producer_seats
schema.description: Number of block producer seats

schema.name: orphan_state_witness_max_size
schema.description: Maximum size of state witnesses in the OrphanStateWitnessPool. We keep only orphan witnesses which are smaller than this size. This limits the maximum memory usage of OrphanStateWitnessPool.

schema.name: orphan_state_witness_pool_size
schema.description: OrphanStateWitnessPool keeps instances of ChunkStateWitness which can't be processed because the previous block isn't available. The witnesses wait in the pool until the required block appears. This variable controls how many witnesses can be stored in the pool.

schema.name: produce_chunk_add_transactions_time_limit
schema.description: Limit the time of adding transactions to a chunk. A node produces a chunk by adding transactions from the transaction pool until some limit is reached. This time limit ensures that adding transactions won't take longer than the specified duration, which helps to produce the chunk quickly.

schema.name: produce_empty_blocks
schema.description: Produce empty blocks, use `false` for testing.

schema.name: protocol_version_check
schema.description: Configures whether the node checks the next or the next next epoch for network version compatibility.

schema.name: rpc_addr
schema.description: Listening rpc port for status.

schema.name: save_invalid_witnesses
schema.description: Save observed instances of invalid ChunkStateWitness to the database in DBCol::InvalidChunkStateWitnesses. Saving invalid witnesses is useful for analysis and debugging. This option can cause extra load on the database and is not recommended for production use.

schema.name: save_latest_witnesses
schema.description: Save observed instances of ChunkStateWitness to the database in DBCol::LatestChunkStateWitnesses. Saving the latest witnesses is useful for analysis and debugging. This option can cause extra load on the database and is not recommended for production use.

schema.name: save_state_changes
schema.description: Whether to persist state changes on disk or not.

schema.name: save_trie_changes
schema.description: save_trie_changes should be set to true iff - archive if false - non-archival nodes need trie changes to perform garbage collection - archive is true, cold_store is configured and migration to split_storage is finished - node working in split storage mode needs trie changes in order to do garbage collection on hot.

schema.name: save_tx_outcomes
schema.description: Whether to persist transaction outcomes to disk or not.

schema.name: save_untracked_partial_chunks_parts
schema.description: Whether to persist partial chunk parts for untracked shards or not.

schema.name: skip_sync_wait
schema.description: Skip waiting for sync (for testing or single node testnet).

schema.name: state_request_server_threads
schema.description: Number of threads for StateRequestActor pool.

schema.name: state_request_throttle_period
schema.description: Number of seconds between state requests for view client. Throttling window for state requests (headers and parts).

schema.name: state_requests_per_throttle_period
schema.description: Maximum number of state requests served per throttle period

schema.name: state_sync
schema.description: Options for syncing state.

schema.name: dump
schema.description: Configures how to dump state to external storage.

schema.name: parts_compression_lvl
schema.description: Zstd compression level for state parts.

schema.name: sync
schema.description: Syncs state from the peers without reading anything from external storage.

schema.name: state_sync_enabled
schema.description: Whether to use the State Sync mechanism. If disabled, the node will do Block Sync instead of State Sync.

schema.name: state_sync_external_backoff
schema.description: Additional waiting period after a failed request to external storage

schema.name: state_sync_external_timeout
schema.description: How long to wait for a response from centralized state sync

schema.name: state_sync_p2p_timeout
schema.description: How long to wait for a response from p2p state sync

schema.name: state_sync_retry_backoff
schema.description: How long to wait after a failed state sync request

schema.name: sync_check_period
schema.description: How often to check that we are not out of sync.

schema.name: sync_height_threshold
schema.description: Sync height threshold: below this difference in height don't start syncing.

schema.name: sync_max_block_requests
schema.description: Maximum number of block requests to send to peers to sync

schema.name: sync_step_period
schema.description: While syncing, how long to check for each step.

schema.name: tracked_shards_config
schema.description: One of multiple possible types

schema.name: transaction_pool_size_limit
schema.description: Limit of the size of per-shard transaction pool measured in bytes. If not set, the size will be unbounded.

schema.name: trie_viewer_state_size_limit
schema.description: Upper bound of the byte size of contract state that is still viewable. None is no limit

schema.name: ttl_account_id_router
schema.description: Time to persist Accounts Id in the router without removing them.

schema.name: tx_routing_height_horizon
schema.description: If the node is not a chunk producer within that many blocks, then route to upcoming chunk producers.

schema.name: version
schema.description: Data structure for semver version and github tag or commit.

schema.name: view_client_threads
schema.description: Number of threads for ViewClientActor pool.

schema.name: dynamic_resharding_dry_run
schema.description: If true, the runtime will do a dynamic resharding 'dry run' at the last block of each epoch. This means calculating tentative boundary accounts for splitting the tracked shards.

rpc-EXPERIMENTAL-congestion-level.description: Measure a single shard's congestion pressure at a chosen block — a 0.0-to-1.0 saturation score.

parameters.name: block_id
parameters.description: Block height (integer) or block hash (string)

parameters.name: shard_id
parameters.description: Shard identifier

parameters.name: light_client_head
parameters.description: Base58-encoded hash

schema.name: inner_rest_hash

schema.name: prev_block_hash
schema.description: Base58-encoded hash

parameters.name: sender_id

parameters.name: transaction_hash
parameters.description: Base58-encoded hash

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Proof subject — `receipt` proves inclusion of a specific receipt produced during execution.

parameters.name: receipt_id
parameters.description: Base58-encoded hash

parameters.name: receiver_id
parameters.description: NEAR account ID

schema.name: inner_lite
schema.description: A part of a state for the current head of a light client. More info [here](https://nomicon.io/ChainSpec/LightClient).

schema.name: inner_rest_hash

rpc-EXPERIMENTAL-protocol-config.description: Fetch the runtime protocol config at a chosen block — gas costs, storage prices, and limits currently in force.

schema.name: avg_hidden_validator_seats_per_shard
schema.description: Expected number of hidden validators per shard.

schema.name: block_producer_kickout_threshold
schema.description: Threshold for kicking out block producers, between 0 and 100.

schema.name: chain_id
schema.description: ID of the blockchain. This must be unique for every blockchain. If your testnet blockchains do not have unique chain IDs, you will have a bad time.

schema.name: chunk_producer_kickout_threshold
schema.description: Threshold for kicking out chunk producers, between 0 and 100.

schema.name: chunk_validator_only_kickout_threshold
schema.description: Threshold for kicking out nodes which are only chunk validators, between 0 and 100.

schema.name: dynamic_resharding
schema.description: Enable dynamic re-sharding.

schema.name: epoch_length
schema.description: Epoch length counted in block heights.

schema.name: fishermen_threshold
schema.description: Amount in yoctoNEAR

schema.name: gas_limit
schema.description: Gas amount

schema.name: gas_price_adjustment_rate
schema.description: Gas price adjustment rate

schema.name: genesis_height
schema.description: Height of genesis block.

schema.name: genesis_time
schema.description: Official time of blockchain start.

schema.name: max_gas_price
schema.description: Amount in yoctoNEAR

schema.name: max_inflation_rate
schema.description: Maximum inflation on the total supply every epoch.

schema.name: max_kickout_stake_perc
schema.description: Max stake percentage of the validators we will kick out.

schema.name: min_gas_price
schema.description: Amount in yoctoNEAR

schema.name: minimum_stake_divisor
schema.description: The minimum stake required for staking is last seat price divided by this number.

schema.name: minimum_stake_ratio
schema.description: The lowest ratio s/s_total any block producer can have. See <https://github.com/near/NEPs/pull/167> for details

schema.name: minimum_validators_per_shard
schema.description: The minimum number of validators each shard must have

schema.name: num_block_producer_seats
schema.description: Number of block producer seats at genesis.

schema.name: num_block_producer_seats_per_shard
schema.description: Defines number of shards and number of block producer seats per each shard at genesis.

schema.name: num_blocks_per_year
schema.description: Expected number of blocks per year

schema.name: online_max_threshold
schema.description: Online maximum threshold above which validator gets full reward.

schema.name: online_min_threshold
schema.description: Online minimum threshold below which validator doesn't receive reward.

schema.name: protocol_reward_rate
schema.description: Protocol treasury rate

schema.name: protocol_treasury_account
schema.description: NEAR account ID

schema.name: protocol_upgrade_stake_threshold
schema.description: Threshold of stake that needs to indicate that they ready for upgrade.

schema.name: protocol_version
schema.description: Current Protocol Version

schema.name: runtime_config
schema.description: View that preserves JSON format of the runtime config.

schema.name: account_creation_config
schema.description: The structure describes configuration for creation of new accounts.

schema.name: congestion_control_config
schema.description: The configuration for congestion control. More info about congestion [here](https://near.github.io/nearcore/architecture/how/receipt-congestion.html?highlight=congestion#receipt-congestion)

schema.name: storage_amount_per_byte
schema.description: Amount in yoctoNEAR

schema.name: transaction_costs
schema.description: Describes different fees for the runtime

schema.name: wasm_config
schema.description: Config of wasm operations.

schema.name: witness_config
schema.description: Configuration specific to ChunkStateWitness.

schema.name: shard_layout
schema.description: One of multiple possible types

schema.name: shuffle_shard_assignment_for_chunk_producers
schema.description: If true, shuffle the chunk producers across shards. In other words, if the shard assignments were `[S_0, S_1, S_2, S_3]` where `S_i` represents the set of chunk producers for shard `i`, if this flag were true, the shard assignments might become, for example, `[S_2, S_0, S_3, S_1]`.

schema.name: target_validator_mandates_per_shard
schema.description: Number of target chunk validator mandates for each shard.

schema.name: transaction_validity_period
schema.description: Number of blocks for which a given transaction is valid

schema.name: chunk_producer_assignment_changes_limit

schema.name: chunk_producer_kickout_threshold

schema.name: max_inflation_rate

schema.name: max_kickout_stake_perc
schema.description: Max stake percentage of the validators we will kick out.

schema.name: min_gas_price

schema.name: minimum_stake_divisor
schema.description: The minimum stake required for staking is last seat price divided by this number.

schema.name: minimum_stake_ratio
schema.description: The lowest ratio s/s_total any block producer can have. See <https://github.com/near/NEPs/pull/167> for details

schema.name: num_block_producer_seats

schema.name: num_blocks_per_year

schema.description: Deprecated.

schema.name: num_chunk_producer_seats
schema.description: Number of chunk producers. Don't mess it up with chunk-only producers feature which is deprecated.

schema.name: online_max_threshold
schema.description: Online maximum threshold above which validator gets full reward.

schema.name: online_min_threshold
schema.description: Online minimum threshold below which validator doesn't receive reward.

schema.name: protocol_reward_rate
schema.description: Protocol treasury rate

schema.name: protocol_treasury_account
schema.description: NEAR account ID

schema.name: protocol_upgrade_stake_threshold
schema.description: Threshold of stake that needs to indicate that they ready for upgrade.

schema.name: protocol_version
schema.description: Protocol version that this genesis works with.

schema.name: shard_layout
schema.description: One of multiple possible types

schema.name: shuffle_shard_assignment_for_chunk_producers
schema.description: If true, shuffle the chunk producers across shards. In other words, if the shard assignments were `[S_0, S_1, S_2, S_3]` where `S_i` represents the set of chunk producers for shard `i`, if this flag were true, the shard assignments might become, for example, `[S_2, S_0, S_3, S_1]`.

schema.name: target_validator_mandates_per_shard
schema.description: Number of target chunk validator mandates for each shard.

schema.name: total_supply
schema.description: Amount in yoctoNEAR

schema.name: transaction_validity_period
schema.description: Number of blocks for which a given transaction is valid

schema.name: use_production_config
schema.description: This is only for test purposes. We hard code some configs for mainnet and testnet in AllEpochConfig, and we want to have a way to test that code path. This flag is for that. If set to true, the node will use the same config override path as mainnet and testnet.

schema.name: validators
schema.description: List of initial validators.

rpc-health.description: Ping a node for liveness — returns `null` on success, an error on unhealthy state.

schema.name: result
schema.description: Health status of the node

rpc-latest-block.description: Fetch the latest final block — finality set automatically, no block ID needed.

rpc-view-state.description: Fetch the raw key-value state a contract has written, optionally filtered by key prefix.

parameters.name: account_id
parameters.description: NEAR account ID

parameters.name: include_proof
parameters.description: Include a Merkle proof for the queried state alongside the values.

parameters.name: prefix_base64
parameters.description: Base64-encoded storage key prefix. Use an empty string (`""`) to remove the prefix filter and return all matching contract state values. Large contracts can produce very large responses when no prefix is set.

schema.name: result
schema.description: Resulting state values for a view state query request

transactions-v0-account.description: Fetch account transaction history with optional filters for signer, success status, and sort order.

parameters.name: account_id
parameters.description: NEAR account to query transactions for (may be a signer, predecessor, receiver, or related party).

parameters.name: desc
parameters.description: Sort newest-first when true; oldest-first when false or omitted.

parameters.name: from_tx_block_height
parameters.description: Inclusive lower bound on the transaction's block height.

parameters.name: is_action_arg
parameters.description: Restrict to transactions where this account appeared in action arguments.

parameters.name: is_any_signer
parameters.description: Restrict to transactions where this account signed either the top-level transaction or a delegated action.

parameters.name: is_delegated_signer
parameters.description: Restrict to transactions where this account signed a delegated action.

parameters.name: is_event_log
parameters.description: Restrict to transactions where this account appeared in a JSON event log.

parameters.name: is_explicit_refund_to
parameters.description: Restrict to transactions where this account was the explicit `refund_to` target of an action receipt.

parameters.name: is_function_call
parameters.description: Restrict to transactions where this account was the target of a function-call action.

parameters.name: is_predecessor
parameters.description: Restrict to transactions where this account was the predecessor of a receipt.

parameters.name: is_real_receiver
parameters.description: Restrict to transactions where this account was the real receiver — excluding relayer receivers and gas refunds.

parameters.name: is_real_signer
parameters.description: Restrict to transactions where this account was the real signer — direct or delegated, excluding relayer signers.

parameters.name: is_receiver
parameters.description: Restrict to transactions where this account received a receipt.

parameters.name: is_signer
parameters.description: Restrict to transactions where this account signed the top-level transaction.

parameters.name: is_success
parameters.description: Restrict to transactions whose execution succeeded (true) or failed/pending (false).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum rows to return in one page (1–200).

parameters.name: resume_token
parameters.description: Opaque pagination token returned on a prior page; omit for the first page.

parameters.name: to_tx_block_height
parameters.description: Exclusive upper bound on the transaction's block height.

transactions-v0-account.response: Account history result set Bad request ClickHouse query failure

transactions-v0-block.description: Fetch a block row by height or hash and optionally expand its transactions and receipts.

parameters.name: block_id
parameters.description: Block height (integer) or base58-encoded block hash (string) to fetch.

parameters.name: with_receipts
parameters.description: Include the block's receipts in the response when true.

parameters.name: with_transactions
parameters.description: Include the block's transactions in the response when true.

transactions-v0-block.response: Block lookup result Bad request ClickHouse query failure

transactions-v0-blocks.description: Fetch a bounded list of indexed blocks ordered ascending or descending.

parameters.name: desc
parameters.description: Sort newest-first when true; oldest-first when false or omitted.

parameters.name: from_block_height
parameters.description: Inclusive lower bound on block height.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum blocks to return in one page (1–100).

parameters.name: to_block_height
parameters.description: Exclusive upper bound on block height.

transactions-v0-blocks.response: Block rows Bad request ClickHouse query failure

transactions-v0-receipt.description: Fetch a receipt by ID along with the transaction that produced it.

parameters.name: receipt_id
parameters.description: Base58-encoded receipt ID to look up.

transactions-v0-receipt.response: Receipt lookup result Bad request ClickHouse query failure

transactions-v0-transactions.description: Fetch up to 20 indexed transactions by hash in one request.

parameters.name: tx_hashes
parameters.description: Up to 20 base58-encoded transaction hashes to fetch in one request.

transactions-v0-transactions.response: Matching transactions Bad request ClickHouse query failure

transfers-v0-transfers.description: Fetch transfer rows for one account with optional direction, asset, amount, and time filters.

parameters.name: account_id
parameters.description: NEAR account to query transfers for (the signer or receiver, depending on `direction`).

parameters.name: asset_id
parameters.description: Asset identifier such as `native:near` for NEAR or `<contract_id>` for fungible tokens.

parameters.name: desc
parameters.description: When true, sort newest-first; when false or omitted, sort oldest-first.

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: Restrict to transfers where the account acts as `sender` or `receiver`; omit for both sides.

parameters.name: from_timestamp_ms
parameters.description: Inclusive lower bound on block timestamp in milliseconds since the Unix epoch.

parameters.name: ignore_system
parameters.description: When true, hide system transfers (validator rewards, implicit account creation, refunds).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of transfer rows to return in one page (1–100).

parameters.name: min_amount
parameters.description: Minimum absolute transfer amount in the asset's base units (e.g. yoctoNEAR), stringified u128.

parameters.name: min_human_amount
parameters.description: Minimum transfer amount in human-readable units (decimals already applied).

parameters.name: min_usd_amount
parameters.description: Minimum transfer amount in USD-equivalent at time of transfer.

parameters.name: resume_token
parameters.description: Opaque pagination token returned as `resume_token` on a prior page; omit for the first page.

parameters.name: to_timestamp_ms
parameters.description: Exclusive upper bound on block timestamp in milliseconds since the Unix epoch.

transfers-v0-transfers.response: Transfer rows for the requested account Invalid request body ClickHouse query failure
